title: eupatorium perfoliatum var. cuneatum
intro: Eupatorium perfoliatum var. cuneatum, also known as white snakeroot, is a plant species native to eastern North America, characterized by its distinctive white flowers and tall, slender stem. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. White snakeroot has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and is also believed to have antibacterial and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of white snakeroot include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health problems, although it is essential to use the plant under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
